mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of low-rate periods or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and construction sector growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of high inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks that erode corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines fail to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: High inflation months are followed by positive 5-day returns in >65% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms a strengthening labor market that supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of observations or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic growth that increases corporate earnings expectations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDP growth periods show negative 20-day returns in >50% of cases or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in industrial production signals resolving supply chain constraints and improving manufacturing output, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production spikes fail to precede positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances.
